Purpose and Scope

This document provides a high-level overview of the OpenCode codebase, describing its purpose as an open-source AI coding agent, the monorepo structure, core architecture patterns, and key capabilities. For detailed information about specific subsystems, see:

  • Repository structure and package dependencies: Repository Structure & Packages
  • Detailed architecture diagrams: Architecture Overview
  • Core application components: Core Application
  • User interface implementations: User Interfaces
  • SDK and API details: SDK & API

What is OpenCode

OpenCode is an open-source AI coding agent that provides an alternative to proprietary solutions like Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. It is designed to be provider-agnostic, supporting a vast array of LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local models (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio), and specialized services. The project emphasizes flexibility, extensibility, and user control.

Key characteristics:

  • Open source: 100% open source under MIT license packages/opencode/package.json6
  • Provider-agnostic: Integrated with a wide array of LLM services via a unified abstraction packages/opencode/package.json78-97
  • Multiple interfaces: Terminal UI (TUI), desktop apps (Electron/Tauri), IDE extensions (VS Code/Zed), and web documentation packages/desktop/package.json15-22 sdks/vscode/package.json25-82
  • LSP integration: Native Language Server Protocol support to provide semantic code understanding packages/opencode/package.json71
  • Client-server architecture: A core server that can run locally or be accessed remotely via an SDK packages/sdk/js/package.json11-18

The codebase is organized as a monorepo containing the core server, multiple client implementations, SDK packages, and supporting infrastructure.

Sources: README.md1-142 packages/opencode/package.json78-97 packages/sdk/js/package.json11-18

Repository Structure

The OpenCode repository is organized as a monorepo with three major subsystems and specialized services. It uses bun as the primary package manager and runtime bun.lock1-5

Package Organization Diagram

Core Platform

The core platform consists of primary packages defined in the workspace:

PackageNamePurpose
packages/opencodeopencodeMain application containing CLI, HTTP server, TUI, session management, provider integration, tool system, and LSP management. packages/opencode/package.json1-170
packages/sdk/js@opencode-ai/sdkTypeScript client SDK exposing OpenAPI-based methods for interacting with the server. packages/sdk/js/package.json1-34
packages/plugin@opencode-ai/pluginPlugin system API and base types for extending OpenCode functionality. packages/plugin/package.json1-49

Sources: bun.lock1-29 packages/opencode/package.json1-170 packages/sdk/js/package.json1-34 packages/plugin/package.json1-49

User Interfaces

OpenCode provides multiple interface options, sharing a common UI foundation:

PackageNameTechnologyPurpose
packages/app@opencode-ai/appSolidJSShared UI business logic and context providers. bun.lock30-69
packages/ui@opencode-ai/uiSolidJSComponent library with message rendering and diff viewers. bun.lock37
packages/desktop@opencode-ai/desktopElectron/TauriNative desktop application. packages/desktop/package.json1-76
packages/web@opencode-ai/webAstroStatic documentation site and landing page. packages/web/package.json1-44
sdks/vscodeopencodeVS Code APIVS Code extension for integrated terminal access. sdks/vscode/package.json1-109

Sources: bun.lock30-82 packages/desktop/package.json1-76 packages/web/package.json1-44 sdks/vscode/package.json1-109

Console Platform

The Console is a managed SaaS offering providing OpenCode Zen and OpenCode Go services:

PackageNamePurpose
packages/console/core@opencode-ai/console-coreDatabase operations (Drizzle ORM) and Stripe integration. packages/console/core/package.json1-52
packages/console/app@opencode-ai/console-appSolidStart frontend deployed to Cloudflare. packages/console/app/package.json1-47
packages/console/function@opencode-ai/console-functionCloudflare Workers handling LLM requests via AI SDK. packages/console/function/package.json1-31

Sources: packages/console/core/package.json1-52 packages/console/app/package.json1-47 packages/console/function/package.json1-31

Core Architecture

Client-Server Communication

OpenCode uses a flexible client-server architecture. All clients communicate through the unified SDK layer which supports both local and remote operation modes.

Key architectural components:

ComponentLocationPurpose
Hono Serverpackages/opencode/src/serverHTTP server exposing REST + SSE endpoints. packages/opencode/package.json36-40
SDK Transportpackages/sdk/js/srcAbstracts communication; includes client.ts and server.ts exports. packages/sdk/js/package.json11-18
Tool Registrypackages/opencode/src/toolExecutes built-in and external tools.

Sources: packages/opencode/package.json1-170 packages/sdk/js/package.json11-18

Key Capabilities

Multi-Provider LLM Support

OpenCode integrates with dozens of LLM providers through a unified abstraction layer, utilizing the Vercel AI SDK and specialized providers:

  • OpenCode services: OpenCode Zen (pay-as-you-go) and OpenCode Go.
  • Major providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google (Gemini/Vertex), Amazon Bedrock, Azure packages/opencode/package.json79-91
  • Specialized providers: Groq, Together AI, Cerebras, Deepinfra, Mistral, Perplexity, xAI packages/opencode/package.json82-97
  • Local models: Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio.

Sources: packages/opencode/package.json78-97

Tool System & LSP

OpenCode provides an extensible tool system for AI agents to interact with code and resources:

  • Built-in tools: bash, edit, read, write, grep, glob, task.
  • LSP Integration: Automatically manages Language Server Protocol servers for 20+ languages, enabling agents to perform semantic analysis packages/opencode/package.json71
  • MCP Integration: Supports Model Context Protocol (MCP) for external resource access packages/opencode/package.json104

Sources: packages/opencode/package.json71-169

Distribution

OpenCode is distributed through multiple package managers and formats:

  • Package Managers: npm, Homebrew, Pacman, AUR, Chocolatey, Scoop, Nix.
  • Containers: Docker (ghcr.io/anomalyco/opencode).
  • Desktop: Electron/Tauri-based native applications for macOS, Windows, and Linux packages/desktop/package.json15-22

Sources: README.md46-142 packages/desktop/package.json1-76 STATS.md1-91

Summary

OpenCode is a flexible, provider-agnostic AI coding agent with multiple interface options and comprehensive integration capabilities. The codebase is organized as a monorepo with clear separation between the core platform, user interfaces, and console services.

Next steps:

  • For package-level details, see Repository Structure & Packages
  • For architectural deep-dives, see Architecture Overview
  • For server implementation details, see Core Application
  • For UI implementation details, see User Interfaces

Repository Structure & Packages

1.1-repository-structure-and-packages

Repository Structure & Packages

This document describes the monorepo organization of the OpenCode repository, including workspace structure, package categories, and inter-package dependencies. OpenCode uses Bun workspaces to manage multiple packages that together provide CLI tools, desktop applications, web interfaces, IDE extensions, and a managed cloud platform.

For information about the architectural patterns and runtime behavior of these systems, see Architecture Overview.

Workspace Organization

OpenCode is organized as a monorepo with workspaces defined in the root configuration bun.lock4-28 The repository contains 20+ packages distributed across three primary directories:

DirectoryPurposePackage Count
packages/Core platform, UI components, and services15 packages
sdks/IDE integrations and extensions2+ packages
RootMain CLI package and build toolingWorkspace root

Workspace Configuration

The root workspace bun.lock5-29 defines shared dependencies and catalog versions that child packages inherit. The catalog system ensures consistent versioning across all packages for shared dependencies like typescript, vite, solid-js, and zod.

Repository Package Hierarchy

Workspace Dependencies

Packages reference each other using the workspace:* protocol packages/opencode/package.json108-110 which Bun resolves to local package paths during development and proper versions during publishing.

Sources: bun.lock1-29 packages/opencode/package.json108-110

Core Platform Packages

The core platform provides the foundational CLI, SDK, and plugin system that all other packages depend on.

opencode

Location: packages/opencode/
Package Name: opencode
Type: Main CLI application and server

The primary package containing the OpenCode server, CLI commands, tool system, and runtime packages/opencode/package.json1-175 This is the main entry point users interact with.

Key Features:

  • CLI commands via bin/opencode packages/opencode/package.json19-21
  • HTTP server with Hono framework packages/opencode/package.json147-148
  • Tool registry and execution engine
  • LSP server management
  • Provider integrations for 20+ LLM services packages/opencode/package.json78-97
  • Session and agent management

Major Dependencies:

  • AI SDK ecosystem: ai, @ai-sdk/* packages/opencode/package.json78-128
  • Effect runtime: effect, @effect/platform-node packages/opencode/package.json101-138
  • UI framework: solid-js, @opentui/solid packages/opencode/package.json119-157
  • Database: drizzle-orm packages/opencode/package.json137

@opencode-ai/sdk

Location: packages/sdk/js/
Package Name: @opencode-ai/sdk
Type: Client SDK library

Provides the JavaScript SDK for interacting with OpenCode servers packages/sdk/js/package.json1-34 Consumed by all client applications and IDE extensions.

Exports packages/sdk/js/package.json11-19:

  • .: Main entry (./src/index.ts)
  • ./client: Client creation utilities (./src/client.ts)
  • ./server: Server-side utilities (./src/server.ts)
  • ./v2/*: Next-generation API with OpenAPI code generation packages/sdk/js/package.json15-18

@opencode-ai/plugin

Location: packages/plugin/
Package Name: @opencode-ai/plugin
Type: Plugin development kit

Defines the plugin API and types for extending OpenCode packages/plugin/package.json1-44 Used by both core and third-party plugins.

Exports packages/plugin/package.json11-15:

  • .: Main plugin interface (./src/index.ts)
  • ./tool: Tool development utilities (./src/tool.ts)
  • ./tui: Terminal UI integration types (./src/tui.ts)

Dependencies packages/plugin/package.json19-23:

  • @opencode-ai/sdk: For client communication
  • zod: Schema validation
  • effect: Functional programming library

This document provides a high-level overview of the OpenCode system architecture, describing how the core components interact to provide an AI-powered coding assistant. It focuses on the backend infrastructure, API layer, and integration points between major subsystems.

For detailed information about the repository structure and package organization, see 1.1 Repository Structure & Packages. For specific subsystem details, refer to 2. Core Application, 3. User Interfaces, and 5. SDK & API.

System Overview

OpenCode is built as a modular, event-driven system centered around the opencode package that manages AI sessions, executes tools, and exposes a REST API. The architecture uses a monorepo structure with clear separation of concerns:

  • Core Backend (packages/opencode): Session/message management, AI provider integration, tool execution, and SQLite persistence via Drizzle ORM. packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts1-10 packages/opencode/src/index.ts35-40
  • API Layer: Effect-based HTTP server with OpenAPI routes and SSE streaming. packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts3-11 packages/sdk/openapi.json233-235
  • Client SDK (packages/sdk/js): TypeScript SDK providing a unified interface for both internal (same-process) and HTTP transport modes, including a generated V2 client. packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts243-250 packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts7-79
  • Frontend Clients: Terminal UI (TUI) running in a worker thread, and specialized protocol handlers like ACP (Agent Client Protocol). packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts141-157 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-165
  • Extensibility: Plugin system, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and Language Server Protocol (LSP) support. packages/opencode/src/project/bootstrap.ts1-15

High-Level Architecture

Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts63-76 packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-130 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts201-211 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts160-165

Request Flow Architecture

A user prompt flows through the system from the CLI or an API endpoint to an AI response, with streaming updates via the GlobalBus. The system utilizes an Effect-based runtime for dependency injection and service management.

Prompt Processing Sequence

Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts63-69 packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts167-174 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts182-192 packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts13-78

Core Component Mapping

The following table maps architectural components to their concrete code implementations:

ComponentPrimary File(s)Key Functions/ClassesPurpose
CLI Entrypointpackages/opencode/src/index.tsyargs parserCLI command registration and bootstrap packages/opencode/src/index.ts69-179
TUI Threadpackages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.tsTuiThreadCommandOrchestrates the TUI worker process and RPC packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts79-115
Project Servicepackages/opencode/src/project/project.tsProject.ServiceCore logic for workspace and session lifecycle packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-132
ACP Agentpackages/opencode/src/acp/agent.tsAgentImplementation of Agent Client Protocol packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-165
Worktree Managerpackages/opencode/src/worktree/index.tsWorktree.ServiceManages git worktrees for isolated agent tasks packages/opencode/src/worktree/index.ts145-154
Persistencepackages/opencode/src/storage/db.tsDatabaseSQLite access via Drizzle ORM packages/opencode/src/index.ts36-40
Instance Bootstrappackages/opencode/src/project/bootstrap.tsInstanceBootstrapOrchestrates per-instance service initialization packages/opencode/src/project/bootstrap.ts38-52

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts69-179 packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-132 packages/opencode/src/worktree/index.ts145-154

Event Bus Architecture

The event bus facilitates real-time communication between the core runtime and connected clients. Events are typed using BusEvent and distributed globally.

Event Flow and Transport

Event SystemImplementation FilePurpose
Global Buspackages/opencode/src/bus/global.tsSingleton bus for cross-service events packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts169-174
BusEventpackages/opencode/src/bus/bus-event.tsSchema definition for typed events packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts62
SSE Endpointpackages/sdk/openapi.jsonStreams events to HTTP clients via /global/event packages/sdk/openapi.json233-235
ACP Eventspackages/opencode/src/acp/agent.tsMaps internal events to ACP notifications packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts193-215

Sources: packages/opencode/src/bus/global.ts1-10 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts179-189 packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts7-80

TUI Architecture

The Terminal User Interface (TUI) operates using a worker-thread model to separate the interactive UI from the heavy-lifting logic and server interactions.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts146-159 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts201-211

Core Application

2-core-application

Core Application

The Core Application is the main OpenCode server package (packages/opencode) that provides the foundational AI agent capabilities. It implements a Hono-based HTTP server with an embedded CLI interface, managing AI conversations (sessions), tool execution, provider integrations, and real-time event streaming. The server can run in-process (local mode) or as a networked service (remote mode), exposing an OpenAPI-compliant REST API.

For specific subsystems, see: CLI commands (CLI Entrypoint & Commands), configuration loading (Configuration System), session lifecycle (Session & Agent System), provider integration (AI Provider & Model Management), tool execution (Tool System & Permissions), HTTP routes (HTTP Server & REST API), event streaming (Event Bus & Real-time Updates), LSP integration (LSP & Code Formatting), plugin system (Plugin System), MCP servers (MCP Integration), skills (Skills & Command System), ACP protocol (ACP (Agent Client Protocol)), and the LLM library (LLM Core Library).

Architecture Overview

The Core Application follows a layered architecture using the Effect library to manage services and side effects. It separates transport (HTTP/stdio/internal), business logic (sessions, agents, projects), and execution (tools, shells).

Core Application Architecture

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts69-179 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-163 packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-132 packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts59-111

Application Lifecycle

The application initializes through a multi-stage bootstrap process that sets up logging, performs database migrations, and routes to the requested CLI command.

Application Initialization Flow

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts90-154 packages/opencode/src/project/bootstrap.ts38-52 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts18-46

The initialization sequence includes:

  1. Logging Setup: packages/opencode/src/index.ts95-103 configures the logging system based on environment and CLI flags.
  2. Database Migration: packages/opencode/src/index.ts118-153 performs a one-time migration from legacy storage to SQLite using JsonMigration.run.
  3. Instance Bootstrapping: For commands requiring a project context, InstanceBootstrap.run packages/opencode/src/project/bootstrap.ts38-52 initializes critical services like LSP, Vcs, FileWatcher, and Plugin.

Core Subsystems

Project & Worktree Management

OpenCode treats directories as Projects. The Project service packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-132 handles discovery, VCS detection (primarily Git), and metadata. The Worktree service packages/opencode/src/worktree/index.ts145-152 allows the agent to create isolated Git worktrees for safe code generation and testing.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts113-132 packages/opencode/src/worktree/index.ts145-152

Session & Agent System

The Session system manages conversation state and message history. In the ACP context, ACPSessionManager maps protocol-level sessions to the underlying OpenCode session architecture packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts The Agent class handles the logic for interacting with LLM providers.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-163 packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts

File Monitoring

The FileWatcher service packages/opencode/src/file/watcher.ts61-65 uses @parcel/watcher to provide real-time updates on filesystem changes. This is integrated into the GlobalBus to notify clients and the agent of external edits or build artifacts.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/file/watcher.ts61-157

CLI Tool Rendering

When running via the CLI (e.g., run command), OpenCode provides visual feedback for tool execution using specialized rendering functions:

CLI Tool Rendering Logic

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts72-101 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts121-137

Key stages:

  1. Tool Invocation: The agent selects a tool (e.g., bash, edit).
  2. Metadata Capture: Tools capture execution context in metadata for UI display.
  3. UI Feedback: The CLI renders specific icons and handles block or inline formatting based on the tool state packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts72-101

Transport & API

OpenCode exposes its functionality through multiple transport layers:

  1. REST API: A Hono server providing OpenAPI-compliant endpoints packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts63-67
  2. ACP (Agent Client Protocol): A JSON-RPC based protocol over stdio, enabling IDEs like Zed to control the agent packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts27
  3. TUI RPC: An internal RPC mechanism between the main TUI thread and the background worker process packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts159-169

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts27 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts50-104 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts201-212

CLI Entrypoint & Commands

2.1-cli-entrypoint-and-commands

CLI Entrypoint & Commands

Relevant source files
  • packages/opencode/BUN_SHELL_MIGRATION_PLAN.md
  • packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts

This document describes the CLI entrypoint, command routing system, bootstrap process, and command implementations. It covers how OpenCode starts, parses arguments, initializes the project instance, and dispatches to various command handlers.

For information about the TUI application itself (UI components, rendering), see Terminal User Interface (TUI) For the HTTP server started by serve and web commands, see HTTP Server & REST API For configuration loading and hierarchy, see Configuration System

Entrypoint Architecture

The CLI entrypoint is located at packages/opencode/src/index.ts. It uses yargs for command-line argument parsing and provides a structured command system with middleware, global options, and error handling.

Main Components

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts1-187

CLI Parser Configuration

The yargs parser is configured at packages/opencode/src/index.ts69-81 with:

ConfigurationValuePurpose
scriptName"opencode"Command name in help output packages/opencode/src/index.ts71
wrap100Terminal width for help text packages/opencode/src/index.ts72
parserConfiguration{ "populate--": true }Enables -- argument separator packages/opencode/src/index.ts70
help / --help / -hEnabledShow help message packages/opencode/src/index.ts73-74
version / --version / -vInstallationVersionShow version number packages/opencode/src/index.ts75-76

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts69-76

Global Options

Global options available for all commands:

OptionTypeChoicesDescription
--print-logsboolean-Print logs to stderr packages/opencode/src/index.ts77-80
--log-levelstringDEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERRORSet logging verbosity packages/opencode/src/index.ts81-85
--pureboolean-Run without external plugins packages/opencode/src/index.ts86-89

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts77-89

Middleware Pipeline

The middleware function runs before any command to initialize logging and perform one-time database migration:

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts90-154

Middleware Steps

  1. Log Initialization packages/opencode/src/index.ts95-103

    • Reads --print-logs flag.
    • Determines log level from --log-level or defaults based on Installation.isLocal().
    • Calls Log.init() with configuration.

  2. Environment Variables packages/opencode/src/index.ts107-116

    • Sets AGENT=1, OPENCODE=1, OPENCODE_PID.
    • Logs version, arguments, and process_role from processMetadata.

  3. Database Migration packages/opencode/src/index.ts118-153

    • Checks for marker file at Global.Path.data/opencode.db using Filesystem.exists().
    • If missing, runs JsonMigration.run() to migrate from legacy JSON storage to SQLite using drizzle packages/opencode/src/index.ts129-145
    • Displays a terminal progress bar showing percentage and current migration label packages/opencode/src/index.ts134-140

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts118-153

Command Registration

Commands are registered using the .command() method at packages/opencode/src/index.ts157-179 The default command (invoked when no command is specified) is TuiThreadCommand.

Registered Commands

CommandModuleDescription
run [message..]RunCommandExecute opencode with a specific message packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts121-122
$0 [project]TuiThreadCommandStart the OpenCode TUI (Default) packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts79-81
serveServeCommandStart the OpenCode server packages/opencode/src/index.ts169
webWebCommandStart the OpenCode web interface packages/opencode/src/index.ts170
acpAcpCommandStart the Agent Client Protocol server packages/opencode/src/index.ts157
mcpMcpCommandManage Model Context Protocol servers packages/opencode/src/index.ts158
agentAgentCommandManage agents and configurations packages/opencode/src/index.ts166

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts157-179

Command Implementation Pattern

All commands follow the cmd() or effectCmd() helper pattern. Each command exports an object with:

PropertyTypeDescription
commandstringCommand name and positional arguments
describestringHelp text description
builderfunctionYargs builder for options and flags
handlerasync/Effect functionCommand implementation logic

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts121-230 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts79-115

TUI Worker Architecture

The TUI runs in a separate worker thread to isolate the UI from the server runtime. This is implemented in TuiThreadCommand.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts146-187 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts44-46

Worker Implementation

The worker initializes its own logging and handles RPC requests from the main thread. It manages an internal event stream that forwards events to the TUI via RPC.

MethodParametersPurpose
fetch{ url, method, headers, body }Proxy HTTP requests to the internal Hono server packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts51-69
server{ port, hostname, mdns, cors }Start the external listener for remote access packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts74-78
reload-Invalidate and reload configuration packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts87-95
shutdown-Dispose instances and stop the server packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts96-101

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.ts50-102

Command Categories

Interactive & Protocol Commands

TuiThreadCommand packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts79-215

  • Default command when no command is specified.
  • Spawns a Worker thread for the server logic packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts146-148
  • Supports relative project paths, session continuation, and forking packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts93-106

AcpCommand packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts13-73

  • Starts the Agent Client Protocol server, enabling OpenCode to function as a backend for editors like Zed.
  • Maps process.stdin and process.stdout to a JSON-RPC stream packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts33-56
  • Initializes an AgentSideConnection using the ACP.init helper packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts57-61

Non-Interactive Execution

RunCommand packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts121-230

  • Executes a prompt directly from the CLI.
  • Supports three modes: Non-interactive (single prompt), Interactive local (--interactive), and Interactive attach (--interactive --attach) packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts3-9
  • Includes a sophisticated tool output formatter that renders icons and status for bash, edit, write, read, glob, grep, etc. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts85-119

Infrastructure & Management

ServeCommand & WebCommand packages/opencode/src/index.ts169-170

  • Starts the headless HTTP server or the server with an embedded Web UI packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/serve.ts14-23 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/web.ts39-83

UpgradeCommand packages/opencode/src/index.ts167

  • Checks for updates and performs upgrades based on the installation method (curl, npm, brew, etc.) packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts173-182

Installation & Upgrade System

OpenCode detects its installation method to provide appropriate upgrade paths.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts144-164 packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts173-182

MethodDetection Logic
curlChecks if process.execPath includes .opencode/bin or .local/bin packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts174-175
npmRuns npm list -g --depth=0 packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts179
brewChecks for opencode in brew list --formula packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts136-142

Sources: packages/opencode/src/installation/index.ts173-200

Error Handling

The CLI implements global error handling at packages/opencode/src/index.ts180-232

  1. Yargs Failures: Catches "Unknown argument" or "Invalid values" to show help packages/opencode/src/index.ts180-191
  2. Fatal Errors: Caught in a global try/catch around cli.parse().
    • NamedError: Extracts structured data using toObject() packages/opencode/src/index.ts206-211
    • Error: Standard JS error handling (name, message, stack) packages/opencode/src/index.ts213-220
  3. FormatError: A specialized formatter converts internal Error objects into human-readable strings for the terminal packages/opencode/src/cli/error.ts17-85

Sources: packages/opencode/src/index.ts180-232 packages/opencode/src/cli/error.ts1-89

Configuration System

2.2-configuration-system

Configuration System

Relevant source files
  • .opencode/opencode.jsonc
  • .opencode/plugins/tui-smoke.tsx

The Configuration System manages hierarchical loading and merging of OpenCode settings from multiple sources with well-defined precedence rules. It handles configuration for providers, agents, tools, permissions, plugins, and UI preferences across global, project, and runtime scopes.

For provider-specific authentication credentials, see the auth system. For runtime flag overrides, see 2.1 CLI Entrypoint & Commands

Configuration Sources and Precedence

OpenCode loads configuration from multiple sources in a specific order, with later sources overriding earlier ones. The system uses deep merging with special handling for array fields like instructions.

Configuration Loading Order (lowest to highest precedence):

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts620-745 packages/opencode/src/config/paths.ts1-125

Precedence Rules

PrioritySourceDescriptionCode Entity
8 (Highest)Managed ConfigEnterprise admin-controlled settingsConfigManaged.systemManagedConfigDir()
7Account ConfigConsole organization settingsAccount.active()
6Inline ConfigOPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT environment variableFlag.OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT
5.opencode DirectoryProject-local configs, agents, pluginsConfigPaths.directories()
4Project Configopencode.json in project rootConfigPaths.projectFiles()
3Custom ConfigPath specified by OPENCODE_CONFIGFlag.OPENCODE_CONFIG
2Global ConfigUser-level configurationGlobal.Path.config
1 (Lowest)Remote ConfigFetched from .well-known/opencode URLAuth.all()

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts620-745 packages/opencode/src/config/managed.ts1-50

Configuration File Locations

OpenCode adheres to platform-specific standards for configuration storage, utilizing Global.Path to resolve base directories.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/paths.ts1-125

Managed Configuration Paths

Managed configurations (enterprise-controlled, highest precedence) are located at system-level paths determined by ConfigManaged.systemManagedConfigDir():

PlatformPath
Linux/etc/opencode
macOS/Library/Application Support/opencode
Windows%ProgramData%\opencode

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/managed.ts1-50

Configuration Schema

The configuration system uses Effect Schema as the canonical source of truth for the Info struct. This schema defines the structure of opencode.json and is used to derive Zod schemas for runtime validation and SDK generation.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts132-175 packages/opencode/src/config/agent.ts23-52 packages/opencode/specs/effect/schema.md143-167

Core Configuration Fields

FieldTypeDescription
$schemastringJSON Schema reference for validation
logLevelLogLevelSystem logging verbosity (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR)
serverConfigServer.ServerConfiguration for serve and web commands
modelConfigModelIDDefault model (e.g., anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet)
permissionConfigPermission.InfoGlobal tool execution rules
pluginConfigPlugin.Spec[]Array of plugin identifiers or local paths
autoupdateboolean | 'notify'Update behavior control
snapshotbooleanEnable/disable filesystem tracking for undo (defaults to true)

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts132-175 packages/opencode/src/config/permission.ts24-46

Configuration Loading Implementation

The configuration loading process is implemented in Config.state() using the InstanceState pattern to ensure reactivity and caching per-project instance.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts620-745

Merge Strategy

The system uses mergeConfigConcatArrays to handle overlapping configuration sources:

  • General Fields: Deep merge via remeda.mergeDeep (later sources override earlier).
  • Instructions: The instructions array is concatenated and deduplicated via Set rather than replaced, allowing cumulative system instructions.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts49-61

Directory-Based Configuration

Beyond the JSON config, OpenCode scans .opencode/ (project) and ~/.config/opencode/ (global) for specialized files:

  • Agents: Markdown files in agents/. The file name becomes the agent name (e.g., review.md creates agent review). Frontmatter defines metadata like model or mode, while the body becomes the system prompt.
  • Commands: Markdown files in commands/ or entries in the command record of opencode.json.
  • Plugins: Supports local paths and npm packages. Specified in opencode.json or placed in plugins/.
  • Skills: Additional folder paths for reusable prompt snippets can be added via the skills field.
  • Backwards Compatibility: The system supports singular names (e.g., agent/) but prefers plural names (agents/) for subdirectories.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts143-160 packages/opencode/src/config/agent.ts110-140 packages/opencode/src/config/entry-name.ts1-20

Configuration API & State

Internal State Management

Configuration is managed as an Effect-based service, providing reactive updates to the application.

  • Config.Service.get(): Retrieves the current merged configuration for the active instance.
  • Config.Service.update(config): Persists updates to the local opencode.json.
  • Config.Service.invalidate(): Clears the configuration cache, forcing a reload on the next access.

Sources: packages/opencode/test/config/config.test.ts64-77 packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts500-550

Validation and Migrations

The normalizeLoadedConfig() function handles legacy configuration keys to maintain backward compatibility:

  • theme, keybinds, and tui keys are stripped from opencode.json with a warning to move them to tui.json.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts63-73

Plugin Spec Resolution

During loading, plugin specifications are normalized relative to the config file that declared them via ConfigPlugin.resolvePluginSpec. This prevents relative paths from being misinterpreted when configs are merged from different filesystem locations.

Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts105-113 packages/opencode/src/config/plugin.ts1-100

Session & Agent System

2.3-session-and-agent-system

Session & Agent System

Relevant source files
  • packages/opencode/migration/20260413175956_chief_energizer/migration.sql
  • packages/opencode/migration/20260413175956_chief_energizer/snapshot.json

  • packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/session/todo.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/storage/json-migration.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/tool/todo.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/v2/session-message-updater.ts
  • packages/opencode/src/v2/session-message.ts

  • packages/opencode/test/image/image.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/reference/reference.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-session.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/server/negative-tokens-regression.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/compaction.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/instruction.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/message-v2.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/processor-effect.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/prompt.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/retry.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/revert-compact.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/schema-decoding.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/session/snapshot-tool-race.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/tool/registry.test.ts
  • packages/opencode/test/v2/session-message-updater.test.ts

The Session & Agent System is the core conversational runtime of OpenCode. It manages conversation threads (sessions), message/part structures, agent configurations (build, plan, explore, etc.), and the agentic execution loop that orchestrates LLM interactions with tools. This system bridges user input to AI-generated responses through a stateful, event-driven architecture powered by the Effect runtime [packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:93-121].

Architecture Overview

The Session & Agent System consists of storage (SQLite database via Drizzle), business logic (session/message/agent managers), and the execution runtime (prompt loop + LLM integration).

Natural Language to Code Entity Mapping

The following diagram maps high-level system concepts to their specific implementation classes and namespaces in the codebase.

Title: "System Concept to Code Entity Mapping"

Sources:

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:17-17]
  • [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:86-250]
  • [packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:85-85]
  • [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:72-72]

Session Lifecycle

Session Structure

A session represents a conversation thread linked to a project and workspace. It uses a descending ULID for the id to ensure reverse-chronological sorting [packages/opencode/src/session/schema.ts:4-4].

FieldTypeDescription
idSessionIDDescending ULID [packages/opencode/src/session/schema.ts:4-4]
projectIDProjectIDLinks session to a project [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:19-19]
parentIDSessionID?Used for child/forked sessions [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:20-20]
permissionRuleset?Session-level permission overrides [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:24-24]
summaryJSONStatistics on file additions, deletions, and changed files [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:22-22]

Data Flow: Session Persistence

Title: "Session Persistence Flow"

Sources:

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/session.sql.ts:17-30]
  • [packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:98-98]

Message & Part System

OpenCode uses a "Part-based" message structure. Instead of a single text string, a message consists of multiple typed parts (text, tool calls, reasoning, etc.).

MessageV2 Structure

Messages are defined using the MessageV2 namespace. Assistant messages track performance metrics like token usage and cost [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:311-325].

Part Types

Parts are discriminated unions based on the type field [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:252-256].

TypeDescription
textStandard text content [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:111-127]
reasoningExtended thinking/thought blocks for models [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:129-141]
toolRepresents a tool call and its execution state [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:258-270]
fileAttached files or media with MIME types [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:185-195]
agentReference to a sub-agent invocation [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:197-204]
compactionMarker for a context compaction event [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:210-217]
snapshotCaptures the state of the worktree [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:92-99]

Sources:

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:79-340]

Agent System

Agents are configured behaviors that define how the LLM interacts with the environment.

Agent Configurations

Agents are defined in Agent.Info and include fields for mode, permission, and prompt [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:31-51].

  • Build Agent: The default agent for tool execution. It has permissions for question and plan_enter [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:121-135].
  • Plan Agent: Specializes in architecture; disallows edit tools except for specific plan files [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:136-158].
  • Explore Agent: Optimized for codebase navigation using grep, glob, and read [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:173-199].
  • General Agent: For researching complex questions and executing tasks in parallel [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:159-172].

Permission Rulesets

Permissions are hierarchical, merging global defaults, agent-specific rules, and user-defined configurations [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:118-132]. Rules define access levels (e.g., allow, deny, ask) [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:97-116]. Default permissions specifically handle sensitive files like .env by setting them to ask [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:112-113].

Sources:

  • [packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:121-199]

Execution Loop & Context Management

The SessionPrompt.loop function is the core of the agentic behavior. It iteratively processes messages until the assistant provides a final answer without tool calls.

The Agent Loop

Title: "Agent Loop Execution (SessionProcessor)"



Context Compaction & Truncation


When a session exceeds the model's context window, SessionCompaction condenses history [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:193-200].


    

  1. Overflow Detection: The system checks token usage against the model's context limit [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:189-192].
    2. 

  2. Summarization: A specialized template creates an "anchored summary" of goals and progress [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:44-79].
    3. 

  3. Pruning: Large tool outputs are truncated to TOOL_OUTPUT_MAX_CHARS (2,000) while protecting tools like skill [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:38-39].
    4. 

  4. Context Injection: The summary is injected into the prompt, allowing the model to retain high-level state while freeing context [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:125-136].
    5. 



The system also implements Truncate.Service to handle massive tool outputs by saving them to temporary files and providing the model with a truncated snippet and a path to the full output [packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:164-173].


Retry Policy


OpenCode implements a robust retry mechanism for transient LLM provider errors [packages/opencode/src/session/retry.ts:174-197].


    

  • Exponential Backoff: Defaults to an initial 2s delay, doubling each attempt [packages/opencode/src/session/retry.ts:24-25].
    • 

  • Header Awareness: Respects retry-after and retry-after-ms headers from providers [packages/opencode/src/session/retry.ts:37-57].
    • 

  • Upsell Handling: Detects usage limits for "OpenCode Go" and provides actionable subscription links [packages/opencode/src/session/retry.ts:74-119].
    • 



Sources:


    

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts:115-202]
    • 

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts:1-186]
    • 

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/retry.ts:33-150]
    • 

  • [packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:135-185]
    • 





Session Summary & Diffing


The SessionSummary service calculates the impact of changes made during a session [packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts:67-71].


    

  • Summarize: Aggregates additions and deletions across all messages and writes to storage [packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts:103-120].
    • 

  • Diffing: Compares worktree states between the start and end of assistant turns [packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts:83-101].
    • 

  • Format Integration: After file writes, the system can trigger the Format.Service to run configured formatters (Ruff, Prettier, etc.) to maintain code style [packages/opencode/src/format/index.ts:196-199].
    • 



Sources:


    

  • [packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts:75-147]
    • 

  • [packages/opencode/src/format/index.ts:32-175]
    • 


    



      

        
AI Provider & Model Management

        
2.4-ai-provider-and-model-management

This document explains how OpenCode integrates with AI providers, discovers and manages model metadata, and routes requests through the provider abstraction layer. It covers the provider initialization system, authentication (OAuth, API keys), model capabilities detection, message transformation, and integration with 20+ LLM services including OpenCode Zen and Go.




Provider Architecture Overview


OpenCode supports a vast array of AI providers through a unified abstraction layer that normalizes differences between provider APIs while preserving provider-specific capabilities like reasoning effort and prompt caching.


Provider System Architecture




Sources: packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts93-118 packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts166-167 packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts150-190




Provider Registry and Initialization


The Provider namespace manages all provider integrations through a centralized registry system. It leverages the Vercel AI SDK for low-level communication while adding a layer of OpenCode-specific logic.


Bundled Providers


OpenCode includes direct imports for common providers in the BUNDLED_PROVIDERS record to ensure stability and performance:



Provider PackageFactory FunctionProvider ID
@ai-sdk/anthropiccreateAnthropicanthropic
@ai-sdk/openaicreateOpenAIopenai
@ai-sdk/googlecreateGoogleGenerativeAIgoogle
@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrockcreateAmazonBedrockamazon-bedrock
@openrouter/ai-sdk-providercreateOpenRouteropenrouter
@ai-sdk/azurecreateAzureazure
@ai-sdk/xaicreateXaixai
gitlab-ai-providercreateGitLabgitlab-ai-provider
@ai-sdk/github-copilotcreateOpenaiCompatiblegithub-copilot


Sources: packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts93-118


Authentication & Connection


OpenCode handles authentication via API keys, environment variables, and OAuth flows.


    

  • API Keys: Stored in ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json after running the /connect command packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx18-21 The Auth.Service provides methods to get, set, and all credentials packages/opencode/src/auth/index.ts44-49
    • 

  • Environment Variables: Providers like Amazon Bedrock fall back to standard AWS environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_PROFILE, AWS_REGION, etc.) if configuration is missing packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx172-185 Configuration file options take precedence over environment variables packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx220-221
    • 

  • OAuth Flows: Managed via plugins and specialized auth handlers. For OpenAI Codex, OpenCode starts a local OAuth server on port 1455 to capture redirect codes packages/opencode/src/plugin/codex.ts14-15 packages/opencode/src/plugin/codex.ts245-246 The Auth.Info schema supports oauth, api, and wellknown types packages/opencode/src/auth/index.ts14-37
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/auth/index.ts59-92 packages/opencode/src/plugin/codex.ts113-145 packages/opencode/src/auth/index.ts14-37




Models.dev Integration


OpenCode uses Models.dev as the source of truth for model metadata, capabilities, and pricing. This allows the client to know exactly what a model can do (e.g., tool calling, image support) without hardcoding every model's features.


Model Metadata and Capabilities


The Model schema defines critical fields for agentic behavior:


    

  • Capabilities: Support for tool_call, attachment, reasoning, and temperature packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts33-36
    • 

  • Cost: Detailed pricing for input, output, cached reads, and cached writes packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts13-26
    • 

  • Limits: Context window size and max output tokens packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts46-50
    • 



OpenCode caches this metadata locally in models.json with a 5-minute TTL (ttl = Duration.minutes(5)) packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts107-111


Model Discovery Flow




Sources: packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts121-128 packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts168-183




Provider Transform Layer


The ProviderTransform namespace is responsible for "massaging" data between OpenCode's internal representation and what specific LLM providers expect.


Message Normalization


The normalizeMessages function handles provider-specific constraints packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts60:


    

  • Anthropic/Bedrock: Filters out empty content messages and empty text parts which cause API errors packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts129-154 packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts157-182
    • 

  • Claude: Scrubs toolCallId to ensure it only contains allowed characters [a-zA-Z0-9_-] packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts184-205
    • 

  • Surrogate Sanitization: Replaces invalid UTF-16 surrogate pairs with \uFFFD to prevent JSON serialization failures packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts22-24
    • 



Request Options


The options function configures provider-specific features like prompt caching packages/opencode/test/provider/transform.test.ts40-47:


    

  • Prompt Caching: Automatically sets promptCacheKey for OpenAI and for other providers when setCacheKey is enabled packages/opencode/test/provider/transform.test.ts72-84
    • 

  • Thinking/Reasoning: Enables thinking configuration for specific providers like zai or zhipuai packages/opencode/test/provider/transform.test.ts159-171
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts60-205 packages/opencode/test/provider/transform.test.ts40-171




OpenCode Zen and Go Services


OpenCode provides managed model access through OpenCode Zen (curated premium models) and OpenCode Go (optimized open-source models).


Zen and Go Implementation


OpenCode Zen and Go are integrated as providers that have been verified to work well with OpenCode's agentic workflows.


    

  • OpenCode Zen: An AI gateway providing access to tested models via curated endpoints packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx51-54
    • 

  • OpenCode Go: A low-cost subscription plan for reliable access to popular open coding models packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx87-90
    • 



Custom Loaders


The opencode custom loader allows access to models with zero input cost even without a key, using a fallback check for auth or config options packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts161-174


Sources: packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx51-115 packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts161-182




Summary of Key Classes & Functions



SymbolFile PathRole
BUNDLED_PROVIDERSpackages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts93Map of supported LLM provider SDK factories.
custom()packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts150Specialized initialization logic for providers.
ModelsDev.Servicepackages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts98Context service for fetching and caching model metadata.
ProviderTransformpackages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts22Utilities for normalizing messages and options across providers.
LLM.runpackages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts76Main execution loop for streaming LLM responses.


Sources: packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts1-200 packages/opencode/src/provider/models.ts1-150 packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts70-200

    



      

        
Tool System & Permissions

        
2.5-tool-system-and-permissions

      

      
Tool System & Permissions

This page documents the tool registry, built-in tools, and the permission system that gates tool execution at runtime in the OpenCode core. It covers the Tool.define API, tool registration, file integrity mechanisms, and the multi-layered permission evaluation logic.




Tool Definition API


All tools are created using the Tool.define utility, which provides a consistent interface for parameter validation and execution context. Tools are defined as Effect generators to manage dependencies like the filesystem or LSP services.


Example Definition:


export const ReadTool = Tool.define(
  "read",
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const fs = yield* AppFileSystem.Service
    // ... setup logic
    return {
      description: DESCRIPTION,
      parameters: Parameters,
      execute: (params, ctx) => Effect.gen(function* () {
        // implementation
      })
    }
  })
)


packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts39-41


The Tool.define wrapper automatically validates parameters against the provided schema packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts99-108 and applies output truncation unless the tool handles it explicitly packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts110-123 It uses Schema.decodeUnknownEffect to ensure the model's JSON output matches the expected tool signature packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts89


Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts130-148 packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts39-41 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts58-69


Tool.Context


Every execute function receives a Tool.Context<M> packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts15-25 as its second argument:



MemberTypePurpose
sessionIDSessionIDCurrent session identifier packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts16
messageIDMessageIDParent assistant message ID packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts17
agentstringName of the active agent packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts18
abortAbortSignalSignal to stop execution if the user cancels packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts19
messagesMessageV2.WithParts[]Full conversation history for context packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts22
metadata(input)FunctionPush live metadata updates to the UI packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts23
ask(req)FunctionRequest a permission gate (allow/deny/ask) packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts24


The ask method is used to verify permissions before sensitive operations. For example, EditTool calls ctx.ask before writing to a file packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts141-149


Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts15-25 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts141-149




File Integrity & Locking


To prevent race conditions and ensure atomic edits, OpenCode employs a file-locking mechanism based on semaphores.


    

  • Locking: The lock(filePath) function retrieves or creates a Semaphore for a specific path packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts37-45
    • 

  • Atomic Edits: EditTool uses withPermits(1) to ensure only one edit operation occurs on a file at a time packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts88-169
    • 

  • BOM Management: Tools use the Bom utility to preserve Byte Order Marks and handle line endings correctly during writes packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts122-131 packages/opencode/src/tool/write.ts46-51
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts37-45 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts88-169 packages/opencode/src/tool/write.ts46-51




Built-in Tools


OpenCode provides a robust suite of tools for repository interaction.



ToolIDPurpose
EditTooleditPrecision string-replacement with line-ending normalization packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts58
ReadToolreadRead files/directories; handles binary detection and PDF sniffing packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts39 packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts108-153
WriteToolwriteCreate or overwrite files packages/opencode/src/tool/write.ts27
TaskTooltaskDelegate tasks to specialized subagents with derived permissions packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts32 packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts76-80
RipgrepripgrepHigh-performance regex search using Ripgrep packages/opencode/src/file/ripgrep.ts143


Tool Implementation Details


Tool Data Flow & Entity Association:




    

  • Read Handling: ReadTool performs binary file detection by checking extensions and non-printable character ratios packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts108-153 It also supports reading directory structures packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts191-200
    • 

  • Edit Strategies: EditTool implements string replacement with normalizeLineEndings packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts22-33 and generates diffs via createTwoFilesPatch packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts133-140
    • 

  • Task Delegation: TaskTool creates a new child Session packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts70-87 and uses ops.prompt to execute the subagent loop packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts123-137 It derives subagent permissions from the parent session and agent config packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts76-80
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts108-200 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts22-140 packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts70-137




Permission System


OpenCode uses a pattern-based ruleset to gate tool execution. Every permission check resolves to allow, deny, or ask.


Permission Evaluation


Permissions are evaluated against the current session's ruleset. When a tool calls ctx.ask, the system checks if the requested action and pattern (e.g., a file path) are permitted for the current agent.


Permission Logic Flow:




External Directory Protection


Access outside the workspace root (Instance.worktree) is strictly controlled.


    

  • Tools call assertExternalDirectoryEffect packages/opencode/src/tool/external-directory.ts15 to verify path safety.
    • 

  • If a path is external, tools like ReadTool or EditTool trigger a ctx.ask for the relevant permission with the specific path pattern packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts182-187 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts141-149
    • 

  • ReadTool uses Reference.Service to bypass checks for files already tracked in the session context packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts178
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts177-187 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts141-149 packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts46-55 packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts179-197




Snapshot & VCS Integration


OpenCode maintains file integrity and allows for "reverts" of tool-induced changes using a Snapshot system.


    

  • Tracking: Snapshot.track() captures the current state of the workspace using a dedicated git-based snapshot directory packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts53 packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts89
    • 

  • Diffing: Snapshot.diffFull() provides granular FileDiff structures containing additions, deletions, and patch text packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts58 packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts22-31
    • 

  • VCS Integration: The VCS service handles batching patches and calculating stats (additions/deletions) for tool results packages/opencode/src/project/vcs.ts154-187
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts16-59 packages/opencode/src/project/vcs.ts154-187




Tool Execution Hooks & Integrity


    

  • Truncation: The Tool.define wrapper uses Truncate.output to prevent large tool responses from exhausting LLM context windows packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts114-123
    • 

  • LSP Integration: After reading or writing files, tools trigger lsp.touchFile to ensure the Language Server is aware of the file state packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts89-91 packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts108-110
    • 

  • Ripgrep Search: The Ripgrep.Service provides a high-performance interface for searching files and generating directory trees packages/opencode/src/file/ripgrep.ts137-141 It handles JSON output parsing and process management packages/opencode/src/file/ripgrep.ts189-191
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts114-123 packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts89-91 packages/opencode/src/file/ripgrep.ts137-191

This page documents the HTTP server implementation and REST API architecture that powers OpenCode's networked functionality. The server provides a comprehensive REST API for managing sessions, projects, configurations, and real-time AI interactions using the Effect-based HttpApi framework.


Purpose and Scope


The HTTP server serves as the central networked interface to OpenCode's core functionality. It exposes endpoints for:


    

  • Session Lifecycle: CRUD operations on sessions, message history, and AI prompting packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/api.ts1-100
    • 

  • Real-time Updates: Event streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE) for UI synchronization packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/event.ts1-50
    • 

  • Provider Management: Discovery of LLM models and credential configuration packages/sdk/openapi.json9-109
    • 

  • System Control: Health checks, upgrades, and global instance disposal packages/sdk/openapi.json194-232
    • 

  • Terminal Integration: WebSocket-based pseudo-terminal (PTY) access for remote shell execution packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/pty.ts72
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts59-156 packages/sdk/openapi.json1-232


Server Architecture


OpenCode uses a modern, type-safe server architecture built on the Effect ecosystem. The server logic is defined using HttpApi, which allows for automatic OpenAPI generation and unified dependency injection across all routes packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts1-157


The server supports two primary execution modes:


    

  1. Network Mode: A real Node.js HTTP server bound to a TCP port using HttpServer.serve packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts62-67
    2. 

  2. Local Transport: A "web handler" that can be called directly via fetch-like requests without network overhead, used primarily for internal communication between the CLI and the server logic packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts40-49
    3. 



Request Flow and Middleware


The server constructs a route tree that applies middleware for security, logging, and workspace routing.


Title: Server Request Flow




Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts91-178 packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts62-76


Code-to-Entity Mapping


This diagram maps system concepts to specific code entities within the server implementation.


Title: Code-to-Entity Mapping




Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/public.ts54-154 packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts114-115 packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/event.ts61 packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts117-124


Route Configuration


The server organizes functionality into logical groups using the HttpApiBuilder.



Path PrefixFunctionImplementation
/globalSystem-wide status, health, and eventsglobalHandlers packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts117
/Control plane and workspace managementcontrolHandlers packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts117
/Session, File, and Provider managementinstanceHandlers, sessionHandlers, etc. packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts130-146
/eventReal-time event streaming (SSE)eventHandlers packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts126
/docOpenAPI specification (JSON)docRoute packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts166-168


Sources: packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts109-178


Middleware and Lifecycle


Authentication


OpenCode implements authorizationRouterMiddleware which verifies credentials. It supports environment-based credentials via OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME and OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-ui.test.ts24-30


CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)


The cors middleware manages cross-origin access. It uses isAllowedCorsOrigin to validate the Origin header against configured CorsOptions packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts100-107


Instance and Workspace Context


The server determines the project context using instanceRouterMiddleware and workspaceRouterMiddleware. These middlewares extract workspace IDs or directory paths from headers like x-opencode-directory to scope operations to specific local projects packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-instance.test.ts54


The WorkspaceRoutingMiddleware also allows for proxying requests to remote targets if the workspace is not local packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/middleware/workspace-routing.ts100-133


Lifecycle Management


The disposeMiddleware ensures that per-request resources are cleaned up. For long-running connections like WebSockets, the WebSocketTracker service manages active sockets and allows for graceful shutdown packages/opencode/src/server/server.ts68-69


Real-time Transport Modes


Server-Sent Events (SSE)


OpenCode uses SSE for unidirectional streaming of session updates and global events.


    

  • /global/event: Streams system-wide updates like installation status packages/sdk/js/src/gen/types.gen.ts10-22
    • 

  • /event: Streams session-specific updates, including AI message deltas (EventMessagePartDelta) and tool progress packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts13-73
    • 



WebSockets


For interactive terminal sessions, the server supports WebSocket upgrades via the /pty/connect route. This allows the TUI or Web interface to maintain a persistent, bidirectional stream for shell interaction packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/pty.ts72


API Specification and SDKs


The REST API is formally defined using OpenAPI 3.1.0 packages/sdk/openapi.json1-6


    

  • Type Generation: Client types and SDK methods are auto-generated from the OpenAPI spec using @hey-api/openapi-ts packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts1
    • 

  • SDK Implementation: The OpencodeClient (v2) provides a structured, class-based interface to the API, supporting both standard HTTP requests and SSE subscriptions packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts243-259
    • 

  • Spec Normalization: The PublicApi implementation includes logic to normalize Effect-generated schemas into the legacy format expected by existing SDK consumers, including stripping optional nulls and collapsing duplicate components packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/public.ts81-107
    • 



Sources: packages/sdk/openapi.json packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/public.ts

    



      

        
Event Bus & Real-time Updates

    •

  • packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/report.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/routing.ts
