    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/v2/session-message-updater.test.ts
    • 





Purpose and Scope


This page documents OpenCode's event-driven architecture, centered around the GlobalBus and instance-specific Bus systems. These components facilitate loose coupling between subsystems (Session, LSP, Config, Tools) and provide real-time state synchronization to clients via Server-Sent Events (SSE). This system is critical for streaming AI responses, updating file tree states, and notifying UIs of background task completions.


Architecture Overview


The event system follows a publish/subscribe pattern where core logic modules publish typed events, and the HTTP server broadcasts these to connected clients. The system is built using the Effect library, leveraging PubSub for asynchronous message passing and Stream for SSE delivery.


Event Flow: From Code Entity to Client




Sources: packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts87-108 packages/opencode/src/bus/global.ts1-30 packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts129-169 packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts187-195 packages/opencode/src/session/projectors.ts68-147


Event Bus Implementation


Core Components



ComponentDefinitionRole
Buspackages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts47Handles events scoped to a specific project directory or Instance. Manages PubSub subscriptions for typed and wildcard events.
GlobalBuspackages/opencode/src/bus/global.ts1-30Singleton for system-wide events. All instance-level events are aggregated here for global broadcasting via GlobalBus.emit.
BusEventpackages/opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts1-15Utility to define type-safe events using Effect Schema for runtime validation.
SyncEventpackages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts68Service that manages persistent events, sequence tracking, and "projectors" that update the database state before broadcasting.
Workspace.Eventpackages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts47-61Specialized events for workspace connectivity status (Ready, Failed, Status).


Instance Bus and Effect Integration


The Bus is implemented as an Effect Service packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts47 It leverages PubSub.unbounded for message distribution within an InstanceState context packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts52-74


When an event is published via the publish function:


    

  1. It retrieves the current InstanceState packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts89
    2. 

  2. The payload is generated with a unique ID using createID() packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts183-185
    3. 

  3. The payload is sent to the typed PubSub for that specific event type if one exists packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts93-94
    4. 

  4. The payload is also sent to a wildcard PubSub for "subscribe all" listeners packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts95
    5. 

  5. Finally, it emits the event to the GlobalBus with directory, project, and workspace metadata to facilitate cross-instance monitoring packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts101-106
    6. 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts87-108 packages/opencode/src/bus/global.ts1-30 packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts183-185


Real-time Synchronization & Batching


The system ensures that clients stay in sync with the server state, particularly during long-running AI operations.


SyncEvent System


The SyncEvent service manages events that must be ordered and persisted. It ensures that events are applied in the correct sequence using an EventSequenceTable packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts78-86


    

  • Run: SyncEvent.run creates a new event, increments the sequence number within a database transaction, and processes projectors packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts129-169
    • 

  • Replay: SyncEvent.replay allows the system to catch up on missed events by verifying sequence numbers packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts72-108
    • 



Session V2 Events


A granular set of V2 events handles high-fidelity session tracking packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts33-270 These include:


    

  • session.next.text.delta: For streaming AI text responses packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts155-163
    • 

  • session.next.tool.input.delta: For real-time visualization of tool argument generation packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts223-232
    • 

  • session.next.step.started/ended: For tracking the lifecycle of individual agent turns packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts101-132
    • 



Workspace Syncing


The Workspace service provides a specialized waitForSync mechanism packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts159-163 It allows clients to block until a workspace has reached a specific event sequence state, using waitEvent to monitor the GlobalBus for completion signals packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts28


Sources: packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts72-169 packages/opencode/src/v2/session-event.ts1-270 packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts150-165


HTTP Server & SSE Streaming


OpenCode uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) to push updates to UIs.


Event Delivery


The server exposes a global event endpoint /global/event packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/index.ts54


    

  1. Transport: The endpoint is consumed as a text/event-stream packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/index.ts61
    2. 

  2. Initial Connection: Upon connection, the stream emits a server.connected event packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/index.ts62
    3. 

  3. Workspace SSE: Remote workspaces can also establish SSE connections via Workspace.connectSSE, which proxies events from remote targets into the local GlobalBus packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts197-200
    4. 



Sources: packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-exercise/index.ts53-65 packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts197-210


Tool Execution & Event Correlation


When tools execute, they utilize the Bus to notify the system of their progress and results, allowing the UI to show active tool use.




    

  1. Tool Trigger: A tool like TodoWriteTool calls a service (e.g., Todo.update) packages/opencode/src/tool/todo.ts42-45
    2. 

  2. Service Publication: The service publishes a BusEvent (e.g., Todo.Event.Updated) packages/opencode/src/session/todo.ts64
    3. 

  3. Global Forwarding: The Bus service dispatches to local subscribers and forwards to GlobalBus with instance context packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts101-106
    4. 

  4. State Persistence: If using SyncEvent.run, projectors automatically update SQL tables (like SessionTable or MessageTable) before the event is broadcast packages/opencode/src/session/projectors.ts69-147
    5. 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts87-108 packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts129-169 packages/opencode/src/session/todo.ts64 packages/opencode/src/session/projectors.ts68-147

    



      

        
LSP & Code Formatting

        
2.8-lsp-and-code-formatting

      

      
LSP & Code Formatting



Relevant source files

    

  • packages/opencode/parsers-config.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/dialog-console-org.tsx
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/util/provider-origin.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/config/console-state.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/config/formatter.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/config/lsp.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/config/plugin.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/config/skills.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/lsp/diagnostic.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/lsp/language.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/lsp/lsp.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/config/lsp.test.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/fixture/lsp/fake-lsp-server.js
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/lsp/client.test.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/lsp/index.test.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/lsp/lifecycle.test.ts
    • 

  • packages/opencode/test/util/effect-zod.test.ts
    • 

  • packages/web/src/content/docs/ecosystem.mdx
    • 

  • packages/web/src/content/docs/formatters.mdx
    • 

  • packages/web/src/content/docs/lsp.mdx
    • 





This document covers OpenCode's Language Server Protocol (LSP) integration and automatic code formatting system. LSP provides code intelligence features (diagnostics, hover information, go-to-definition, etc.) that enhance the AI agent's understanding of code. The formatter system automatically applies language-specific formatting to files after edits, ensuring consistency with project style conventions.




LSP Architecture


OpenCode manages multiple LSP servers per project, automatically spawning and configuring them based on file extensions and project structure. The system consists of three layers: server definitions, client connections, and operation handlers.


System Components




Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/lsp.ts152-220 packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts58-64 packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts31-50




LSP Server Definitions


Each LSP server is defined by an LSPServer.Info interface specifying its identifier, supported extensions, root detection logic, and spawn function. OpenCode includes 20+ built-in server definitions with automatic download capabilities.


Server Information Structure



FieldTypeDescription
idstringUnique server identifier (e.g., "typescript", "eslint")
extensionsstring[]File extensions handled by this server
rootRootFunctionAsync function to detect project root from file path
spawnFunctionAsync function returning Handle with process and initialization options


Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts58-64


Root Detection Strategies


LSP servers identify the project root using the NearestRoot helper, which searches upward from a file for specific marker files while respecting exclusions (e.g., ignoring Deno roots for TypeScript projects).




Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts34-56 packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts96-99


Auto-Download Mechanism


OpenCode automatically installs missing LSP servers to Global.Path.bin. This is controlled by the Flag.OPENCODE_DISABLE_LSP_DOWNLOAD environment variable.


Example: ESLint Server
The system downloads the vscode-eslint source from GitHub, extracts it using Archive.extractZip, and runs npm install and npm run compile to build the server locally.
Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts161-195


Example: Vue Language Server
If vue-language-server is not in PATH, it attempts to resolve it via Npm.which("@vue/language-server").
Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts129-136




LSP Client Communication


OpenCode creates LSPClient.Info instances for each unique combination of server and project root. Clients communicate with servers using JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout pipes via vscode-jsonrpc.


Client Lifecycle




Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts141-207 packages/opencode/src/lsp/lsp.ts222-240


Diagnostics Flow


The client listens for textDocument/publishDiagnostics notifications. When received, it updates a local Map<string, Diagnostic[]> and publishes a Bus.publish(Event.Diagnostics, ...) event to notify the system.


Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts160-173 packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts180-197




Code Formatting System


OpenCode automatically runs language-specific formatters after file edits. The system manages a registry of formatters that check for project-specific configurations before running.


Formatter Registry



FormatterExtensionsCommand ExampleEnabled Check
prettier.js, .ts, .css, .json, .mdprettier --write $FILEFinds package.json with prettier dep packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts70-83
biome.js, .ts, .json, .cssbiome format --write $FILEFinds biome.json or biome.jsonc packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts142-152
ruff.py, .pyiruff format $FILEChecks for ruff binary and pyproject.toml packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts188-215
gofmt.gogofmt -w $FILEChecks if gofmt exists in PATH packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts17-25
zig.zig, .zonzig fmt $FILEChecks if zig exists in PATH packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts155-163
uv.py, .pyiuv format -- $FILEChecks if uv exists and ruff is disabled packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts235-246


Sources: packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts17-260


Formatter Selection Logic


Formatters replace the $FILE placeholder in their command array with the actual file path before execution. Matching formatters are run sequentially.




Sources: packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts14-15 packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts17-260




Configuration


Both LSP and Formatters are configurable via opencode.json. Users can disable specific servers, override commands, or add custom environment variables.


Configuration Schema Examples


LSP Customization:


{
  "lsp": {
    "rust": {
      "env": { "RUST_LOG": "debug" }
    },
    "eslint": { "disabled": true }
  }
}


Sources: packages/web/src/content/docs/lsp.mdx96-107 packages/opencode/src/config/lsp.ts12-21


Feature Flags


    

  • OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_OXFMT: Enables the experimental oxfmt formatter for JS/TS. packages/opencode/src/format/formatter.ts93
    • 

  • OPENCODE_DISABLE_LSP_DOWNLOAD: Prevents the system from fetching binaries from the internet. packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts132 packages/opencode/src/lsp/server.ts163
    • 

  • OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TY: Disables pyright in favor of experimental ty server. packages/opencode/src/lsp/lsp.ts114-124
    • 





Performance & Reliability


    

  • Diagnostics Debounce: The system uses a 150ms debounce period when waiting for diagnostics to handle rapid-fire notifications. packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts17
    • 

  • Timeout Protection: LSP initialization is wrapped in a 45,000ms timeout to prevent hanging on broken servers. packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts22
    • 

  • Diagnostic Requests: The client supports specific timeouts for document diagnostics (5,000ms) and full workspace diagnostics (10,000ms). packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts18-19
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/lsp/client.ts17-22

    



      

        
Plugin System

        
2.9-plugin-system

This page describes the OpenCode plugin architecture: how plugins are discovered, loaded, initialized, and how they interact with core systems via hooks. It covers the internal machinery in packages/opencode and the public API surface in packages/plugin.




Overview


Plugins are JavaScript/TypeScript modules that export functions conforming to the Plugin type. Each plugin receives a PluginInput context—containing an SDK client, project metadata, and a shell reference—and returns a Hooks object. Hooks are callbacks called at defined points: before/after tool execution, during LLM parameter resolution, or upon system events.


The system supports three primary loading patterns:



SourceLoading MechanismConfiguration
InternalStatically imported in Plugin.ServiceHardcoded in INTERNAL_PLUGINS packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts59-67
npmDownloaded and installed via Bunopencode.json plugin array packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts160
LocalScanned from filesystem.opencode/plugins/ or ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx57-59


Sources: packages/plugin/src/index.ts75-81 packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts160 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts59-67 packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx57-59




Architecture & Data Flow


The plugin system is managed as an Effect service. It maintains a state of active hooks collected from all initialized plugins. The ConfigPlugin.resolvePluginSpec function ensures that plugin paths are normalized relative to the configuration file that declared them.


Figure: Plugin System Architecture




Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts105-113 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts42-56 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts108-164




Plugin Loading Pipeline


Loading is orchestrated by the PluginLoader. When a session initializes, it performs these steps:


    

  1. Normalization: ConfigPlugin.resolvePluginSpec takes a plugin string (e.g., ./my-plugin.ts or npm-package) and converts it into a ConfigPlugin.Spec. packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts105-113
    2. 

  2. Context Preparation: Generates a PluginInput containing a client (SDK), project info, directory, worktree, and a $ (BunShell) for secure shell access. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts132-147
    3. 

  3. Dependency Resolution: If an npm plugin is specified, the system uses the Npm service to ensure the package is installed. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts182-185
    4. 

  4. Module Evaluation: The loader supports multiple formats:

      

    • V1 Plugin Objects: Objects containing an id and a server property. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts95-101
      • 

    • Legacy Plugins: Modules that export one or more functions as plugin entrypoints. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts80-93
      • 

    

    5. 

  5. Initialization: Internal plugins (like CodexAuthPlugin or AzureAuthPlugin) are initialized first, followed by external ones. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts149-158
    6. 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts105-113 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts132-147 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts95-106




Hook System


The Hooks interface defines the interaction points between OpenCode and external code.


Figure: Trigger Logic and Hook Associations




Core Hooks



Hook NamePurposeData Structure
chat.paramsModifies LLM parameters (temperature, topP, maxTokens) before request.(input: ChatParamsInput, output: ChatParamsOutput) packages/plugin/src/index.ts246-255
chat.headersInjects custom HTTP headers into LLM provider requests.(input: ChatHeadersInput, output: { headers: Record<string, string> }) packages/plugin/src/index.ts256-259
tool.execute.beforeModifies tool arguments or blocks execution by throwing an error.(input: ToolExecInput, output: ToolExecOutput) packages/plugin/src/index.ts275-278
authDefines custom OAuth or API key authentication logic.AuthHook packages/plugin/src/index.ts228
eventGlobal listener for any system event published on the GlobalBus.(input: { event: Event }) packages/plugin/src/index.ts223
permission.askCustom logic to handle tool permission requests.(input: Permission, output: { status: "ask" | "deny" | "allow" }) packages/plugin/src/index.ts260
experimental.chat.system.transformModifies the system prompt before it is sent to the LLM.(input: { sessionID: string, model: Model }, output: { system: string[] }) packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts118-122


Sources: packages/plugin/src/index.ts222-280 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts37-41 packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts118-122




Plugin Development Patterns


Custom Tool Definition


Plugins can define custom tools using the tool() helper. These tools specify a description, args (via Zod schema), and an execute function. If a custom tool name (derived from filename) matches a built-in tool, it overrides it.


import { tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
 
export default tool({
  description: "Query the project database",
  args: {
    query: tool.schema.string().describe("SQL query to execute"),
  },
  async execute(args) {
    // Your database logic here
    return `Executed query: ${args.query}`
  },
})


Sources: packages/plugin/src/tool.ts1-20 packages/plugin/src/index.ts17-19


Environment Injection


Plugins can inject environment variables into all shell executions (both AI tools and user terminals) using the shell.env hook.


export const InjectEnvPlugin = async () => {
  return {
    "shell.env": async (input, output) => {
      output.env.MY_API_KEY = "secret"
      output.env.PROJECT_ROOT = input.cwd
    },
  }
}


Sources: packages/plugin/src/index.ts279-282


Dependency Management


The configuration system supports resolving plugin dependencies via the Npm service. If a plugin spec refers to an npm package, OpenCode ensures it is installed in the local environment before initialization.


Sources: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts45 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts182-185




Technical Details: Persistence & Precedence


    

  • Managed Configuration: System-wide plugins and settings can be enforced via a "managed" configuration directory (e.g., /etc/opencode/ on Linux), which takes the highest precedence and cannot be overridden by users. packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx154-168
    • 

  • Loading Order: Config sources are loaded in a specific sequence: Remote -> Global -> Custom -> Project -> .opencode -> Inline -> Managed. packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx42-55
    • 

  • Trigger Mechanism: The Plugin.trigger method iterates through all registered hooks for a specific event/hook name and executes them sequentially, allowing each hook to modify the output object. packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts42-51
    • 

  • System Prompt Modification: The LLM.run generator calls plugin.trigger("experimental.chat.system.transform", ...) to allow plugins to mutate the system prompt array before it is merged into the final message history. packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts118-122
    • 



Sources: packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx154-168 packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx42-55 packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts42-51 packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts118-122

2.10-mcp-integration

    •

  • packages/opencode/test/mcp/oauth-browser.test.ts

This page documents how OpenCode connects to Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, how server configuration works, how OAuth-based authentication flows are handled, and how tools, prompts, and resources from MCP servers are surfaced to agents and users.




Overview


The MCP namespace manages integration with external MCP servers, enabling OpenCode to access resources (databases, APIs) and invoke tools provided by those servers. Key responsibilities include:


    

  • Connecting to multiple MCP servers (local subprocess, remote HTTP, or SSE) at startup. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts2-5
    • 

  • Maintaining per-server connection status and health using a discriminated union of states (connected, disabled, failed, needs_auth, needs_client_registration). packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts84-107
    • 

  • Converting MCP tool definitions into executable AI tools for the agent via convertMcpTool. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts165-193
    • 

  • Exposing MCP prompts as session commands (slash commands in TUI) by fetching and mapping them to the Command registry. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts117
    • 

  • Exposing MCP resources (URIs and descriptions) for easy reference. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts49-58
    • 



MCP servers are initialized based on the opencode.json configuration and are integrated into the AppRuntime for global access. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts20-21


Sources: packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts1-110 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts1-107 packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts1-50


Diagram: MCP Namespace – Major Components




Sources: packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts101-110 packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts32-50 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts68-80




Configuration


MCP servers are declared under the mcp key in opencode.json. The configuration is handled by ConfigMCP.Info, which distinguishes between local and remote server types. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts48-56


Server Types


OpenCode supports two primary MCP server connection types:


Local Servers (packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts160):


    

  • Executed as local subprocesses.
    • 

  • Defined by a command array (e.g., ["npx", "server-name"]).
    • 

  • Communicates over StdioClientTransport. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts5
    • 



Remote Servers (packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts53-56):


    

  • Accessed via HTTP or Server-Sent Events (SSE).
    • 

  • Defined by a url.
    • 

  • Supports optional oauth configuration for protected resources. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts62-66
    • 



Example Config:


{
  "mcp": {
    "mcp_everything": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
    },
    "jira": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://jira.example.com/mcp",
      "oauth": {
        "scope": "read:jira-work"
      }
    }
  }
}


Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts46-66 packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts1-35




Connection Architecture


All configured servers are connected and tracked via the MCP.Service. The service manages the lifecycle of Client instances from the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts2-5


Diagram: Connection State Machine per MCP Server




Sources: packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts84-107 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts130-164




OAuth Authentication Flow


OpenCode implements Dynamic Client Registration for remote MCP servers if a clientId is not provided in the configuration. packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.ts55-81


    

  1. Detection: If a remote server requires OAuth, the status is flagged as needs_auth. packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts93
    2. 

  2. Provider: McpOAuthProvider implements the OAuthClientProvider interface from the MCP SDK to manage metadata and token exchange. packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.ts28-35
    3. 

  3. Callback Server: McpOAuthCallback starts a local HTTP server (default port 19876) to receive the authorization code. packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.ts141-170
    4. 

  4. Storage: McpAuth.Service persists tokens and client credentials locally in mcp-auth.json (located in Global.Path.data). packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts32-50
    5. 

  5. Token Management: The service provides methods to check expiry and update access/refresh tokens. packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts105-138
    6. 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts7-50 packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-provider.ts28-193 packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.ts141-185




CLI Management


The mcp command group in the CLI provides tools for managing these integrations. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts95-107


    

  • opencode mcp list: Displays configured servers, their connection status (e.g., connected, needs authentication), and their source URL/command. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts109-168
    • 

  • opencode mcp auth [name]: Initiates the OAuth flow for a specific remote server, opening the browser for user consent. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts170-220
    • 

  • opencode mcp logout [name]: Removes stored tokens for a server via McpAuth.remove. packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts83-87
    • 

  • opencode mcp add: (Interactive) Helper to add new MCP server configurations to opencode.json. packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts100
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts95-220 packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts34-48




Diagrams: Natural Language Space to Code Entity Space


This diagram maps how a user's intent to interact with an MCP server travels from a CLI command to the internal service logic.


Diagram: MCP CLI Interaction Path




Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/mcp.ts95-220 packages/opencode/src/mcp/auth.ts50


Diagram: MCP Resource and Tool Discovery


This diagram shows how external MCP capabilities are ingested into the OpenCode runtime.




Sources: packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts49-58 packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts165-193

    



      

        
Skills & Command System

        
2.11-skills-and-command-system

      

      
Skills & Command System

    •

    • 

  • packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx

  • packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

The Skills & Command System provides extensible prompt templates and specialized workflows for common development tasks. Commands are reusable prompt templates with variable substitution, while skills are self-contained instruction sets for domain-specific tasks. Both are invoked via /command-name syntax in the TUI and are managed by the Command.Service and Skill.Service.


System Overview


The command and skill systems serve different but complementary purposes:


    

  • Commands are prompt templates with variable substitution ($ARGUMENTS, $1, $2, etc.) that provide reusable workflows. They can originate from built-in definitions, configuration files, MCP prompts, or skills packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts32-42
    • 

  • Skills are domain-specific instruction sets stored as markdown files (usually SKILL.md). They provide detailed workflows and are loaded dynamically by the Skill.Service packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts147-158
    • 

  • Specialized Agents are AI configurations (primary or subagents) that have specific prompts and tool permissions packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx6-13
    • 



Command & Skill Architecture




Diagram: Command & Skill Architecture

Sources: packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts71-179 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/autocomplete.tsx15-20 packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx42-59


Command Registry


The Command.Service maintains the central registry of available slash commands. It aggregates commands from multiple providers during initialization packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts71-77


Command Types and Templates


Commands are defined using the Info schema, which includes the command name, description, optional agent/model overrides, and a prompt template packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts32-42



SourceDescriptionImplementation
Built-inCore commands like init and review.packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts83-101
ConfigCustom commands defined in opencode.json.packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts103-116
MCPPrompts exposed by Model Context Protocol servers.packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts118-145
SkillDomain-specific workflows from skill files.packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts147-158


Variable Substitution


The system parses templates for placeholders like $1, $2, or $ARGUMENTS to generate interactive hints in the TUI packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts49-57


Specialized Agents


Agents are specialized AI assistants configured for specific tasks. They are defined in opencode.json or via Markdown files in agents/ directories packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx148-194



Agent TypeModeDescriptionTool Access
BuildprimaryDefault agent for development packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx49-53Full access
PlanprimaryRestricted agent for analysis packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx57-68ask for edits/bash
GeneralsubagentResearch and multi-step tasks packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx71-77Full (except todo)
ExploresubagentRead-only codebase exploration packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx79-84Read-only
ScoutsubagentExternal docs/dependency research packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx87-92Read-only


Sources: packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx16-118


TUI Integration


The Terminal User Interface (TUI) provides the interactive environment where commands and agents are invoked via the Prompt component packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/index.tsx136


Autocomplete and Slash Commands


The Autocomplete component monitors the prompt input for trigger characters:


    

  • /: Triggers command autocomplete using data from Command.Service packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/autocomplete.tsx93
    • 

  • @: Triggers agent autocomplete to mention specialized subagents packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/autocomplete.tsx93
    • 





Diagram: UI to Registry Mapping

Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/autocomplete.tsx81-95 packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sync.tsx40-108


Keybinding Integration


Keybindings are managed via OpencodeKeymapProvider and customized in tui.json packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/app.tsx189 packages/web/src/content/docs/keybinds.mdx8-14


    

  • Agent Switching: agent_cycle (default tab) cycles through primary agents packages/web/src/content/docs/keybinds.mdx66
    • 

  • Command Palette: command_list (default ctrl+p) opens the interactive command picker packages/web/src/content/docs/keybinds.mdx23
    • 

  • Session Navigation: session_child_first (<leader>down) and session_parent (up) allow moving between parent and subagent sessions packages/web/src/content/docs/keybinds.mdx49-52
    • 



Permission System


When commands or agents attempt to use tools, the system checks against the session's permissions. If a tool is set to ask, the PermissionPrompt is rendered in the TUI to request user approval packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/permission.tsx115


    

  • Allow/Deny: Users can approve or reject a specific tool call packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/permission.tsx172-188
    • 

  • Always Allow: Users can grant persistent permission for specific patterns packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/permission.tsx142-166
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/permission.tsx1-213 packages/web/src/content/docs/permissions.mdx1-20

    



      

        
ACP (Agent Client Protocol)

        
2.12-acp-(agent-client-protocol)

The Agent Client Protocol (ACP) is a JSON-RPC based communication standard used to integrate OpenCode with external editors and IDEs. It allows a client (the IDE) to control an agent (OpenCode) over stdio, enabling features like session management, capability negotiation, and real-time tool execution feedback. OpenCode serves as a reference implementation for ACP, providing deep integration with editors like Zed, JetBrains, and Neovim.


Protocol Implementation


OpenCode implements the ACP server in the packages/opencode/src/acp directory. It utilizes the @agentclientprotocol/sdk to handle the underlying JSON-RPC handshake, message validation, and type safety packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts1-34


The entry point for the ACP server is the opencode acp command. This command bootstraps the OpenCode environment and starts the process as an ACP-compatible subprocess that communicates with the parent editor over JSON-RPC via stdio packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts13-22


ACP Server Architecture


The implementation follows a modular design to separate protocol handling from OpenCode's internal logic:


    

  • ACP.Agent (agent.ts): Implemented by the Agent class, which satisfies the ACPAgent interface. It handles initialization, processes prompts, and manages the event loop for the protocol packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-165
    • 

  • ACPSessionManager (session.ts): Manages the mapping between ACP session IDs and internal OpenCode session states, including working directory (cwd), model configurations, and variants packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts8-20
    • 

  • AcpCommand (acp.ts): Sets up the transport layer using ndJsonStream over process.stdin and process.stdout to provide the communication channel for the protocol packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts33-61
    • 



Data Flow: IDE to OpenCode


The following diagram illustrates how a request from an IDE flows through the ACP implementation into the core OpenCode logic.


ACP Request Flow




Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts33-61 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-165 packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts8-20


Session & Capability Management


The Agent class in the ACP module manages the lifecycle and state of sessions as requested by the client packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts143-165 It coordinates with the internal OpenCode SDK to bridge high-level protocol requests to system actions.



FeatureCode EntityDescription
Session TrackingACPSessionManagerMaps ACP sessionId to OpenCode's internal sessions and tracks cwd packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts8-20
Usage TrackingsendUsageUpdateCalculates token usage (input + cache) and total cost for the session, pushing updates via sessionUpdate packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts85-133
Event SubscriptionrunEventSubscriptionSubscribes to sdk.global.event() to forward internal OpenCode events to the client packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts176-191
Permission NegotiationpermissionOptionsOffers "Allow once", "Always allow", and "Reject" options to the client packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts153-157
Context LimitsgetContextLimitFetches provider-specific context limits to ensure the IDE can display accurate usage bars packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts66-83


Sources: packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts66-133 packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts8-20


Tool Execution & Permissions


ACP requires explicit handling of tool permissions and execution feedback. When OpenCode's internal engine (e.g., the build or plan agent) asks for permission, the ACP agent intercepts this and forwards it to the IDE packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts195-212


    

  1. Permission Interception: The agent listens for permission.asked events via the global event subscription packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts195-198
    2. 

  2. Client Request: It calls connection.requestPermission with metadata and tool details, including the toolCallId and kind packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts205-215
    3. 

  3. Queueing: Permissions for a specific session are queued using permissionQueues to ensure sequential handling and prevent race conditions packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts200-202
    4. 



Natural Language to Code Entity: Tool Permission Flow




Sources: packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts195-215 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts153-157 packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts25-26


Editor Integration


OpenCode can be integrated into various editors by configuring them to run opencode acp. This starts the OpenCode process in a specialized mode where it expects JSON-RPC input on stdin and provides outputs on stdout.


Zed Integration


Zed uses agent_servers in its settings to spawn OpenCode as an external agent.


"agent_servers": {
  "OpenCode": {
    "command": "opencode",
    "args": ["acp"]
  }
}


Protocol Details


    

  • Environment: The OPENCODE_CLIENT environment variable is set to "acp" during command execution to signal the internal system of the protocol mode packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts24
    • 

  • Transport Layer: The AcpCommand uses ndJsonStream to wrap process.stdin and process.stdout, providing a clean interface for the AgentSideConnection packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts33-56
    • 

  • Agent Configuration: ACP leverages the same agent definitions (like build, plan) defined in the core agent service packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts44-50
    • 



Sources: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/acp.ts13-73 packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts1-34 packages/opencode/src/acp/session.ts1-20

The LLM Core Library is a standalone, Effect-based client library designed to provide a unified, schema-first interface for interacting with Large Language Models (LLMs). While the main opencode package integrates with the Vercel AI SDK for high-level agent workflows, the packages/llm library serves as the foundational layer for model abstraction, protocol handling, and provider-specific transformations using a pure functional approach.


Architecture & Data Flow


The library follows a functional effect-oriented architecture using the effect library. It abstracts the complexities of different provider APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock, Google, etc.) into a consistent internal model defined by LLMRequest and LLMEvent.


Provider Execution Flow


The following diagram illustrates how a request flows through the protocol handlers to specific LLM implementations.


LLM Protocol Pipeline




Sources: packages/llm/src/protocols/anthropic-messages.ts22-24 packages/llm/src/protocols/openai-responses.ts22-24 packages/llm/src/protocols/bedrock-converse.ts22-24 packages/llm/src/protocols/gemini.ts20-21


Protocol Implementations


Each provider implementation in packages/llm/src/protocols is responsible for "lowering" the generic LLMRequest into a provider-specific JSON body and "lifting" the raw response stream into a unified LLMEvent stream.


Key Protocol Handlers



Protocol FileAdapter NameKey Features
anthropic-messages.tsanthropic-messagesSupports interleaved thinking, ephemeral cache hints, and tool streaming packages/llm/src/protocols/anthropic-messages.ts29-54
openai-responses.tsopenai-responsesUnified HTTP and WebSocket transport for the /responses API packages/llm/src/protocols/openai-responses.ts71-75
bedrock-converse.tsbedrock-converseSigV4 signing via aws4fetch and media block handling packages/llm/src/protocols/bedrock-converse.ts31-41
gemini.tsgeminiSpecialized tool-schema sanitization for Google's strict JSON Schema dialect packages/llm/src/protocols/gemini.ts146-162


Schema-First Request Model


The library utilizes effect/Schema to ensure type safety across the LLM boundary.


    

  • Request Body: Each protocol defines a *Body schema (e.g., AnthropicMessagesBody) that validates the outgoing JSON packages/llm/src/protocols/anthropic-messages.ts143-144
    • 

  • Streaming Events: Responses are parsed into a discriminated union of events such as text-delta, tool-call, and request-finish packages/llm/src/schema/events.ts1-20
    • 



Entity Association: Code Entities to Protocols




Sources: packages/llm/src/protocols/anthropic-messages.ts29-32 packages/llm/src/protocols/bedrock-converse.ts35-39 packages/llm/src/protocols/openai-responses.ts105-112 packages/llm/src/protocols/gemini.ts146-151


HTTP Recording & Deterministic Testing


The @opencode-ai/http-recorder package provides a robust mechanism for recording and replaying LLM interactions. This ensures that tests are deterministic and do not drift from upstream API changes.


Record/Replay Modes


The recorder supports four primary modes via RecordReplayMode:


    

  • auto: Replay if the cassette exists, otherwise record. CI=true forces replay packages/http-recorder/src/recorder.ts25-29
    • 

  • record: Execute upstream and overwrite the cassette packages/http-recorder/src/effect.ts91
    • 

  • replay: Strict matching against recorded interactions packages/http-recorder/src/effect.ts115
    • 

  • passthrough: Bypass the recorder packages/http-recorder/src/effect.ts89
    • 



Redaction & Secret Safety


To prevent leaking credentials in version-controlled cassettes, the recorder applies aggressive redaction:


    

  • Header Redaction: Strips sensitive headers like authorization and x-api-key packages/http-recorder/src/redaction.ts3-11
    • 

  • Query Redaction: Replaces parameters like api_key and signature with [REDACTED] packages/http-recorder/src/redaction.ts13-26
    • 

  • Secret Scanning: Scans all strings for patterns matching Bearer tokens, OpenAI keys, and AWS credentials packages/http-recorder/src/redaction.ts28-36 If a secret is detected, it triggers an UnsafeCassetteError and refuses to write the file packages/http-recorder/src/recorder.ts7-16
    • 



Effect Integration


The recorder is provided as an Effect layer cassetteLayer(name), which replaces the standard HttpClient with a recording-aware version packages/http-recorder/src/effect.ts139-144


Sources: packages/http-recorder/README.md44-50 packages/http-recorder/src/effect.ts61-64 packages/http-recorder/src/recorder.ts31-43


Comparison: LLM Core vs. AI SDK Integration


The packages/llm library differs significantly from the AI SDK integration found in the main opencode package:



FeatureLLM Core Library (packages/llm)AI SDK Integration (opencode/src/provider)
PhilosophyEffect-based, functional, statelessHigh-level agentic framework
Protocol SupportRaw protocol lowering (Bedrock Converse, etc.)High-level model abstraction
TransportCustom HTTP/WS transport handlersVercel AI SDK transport layers
Testinghttp-recorder cassettesMocking / Live integration tests
Tool CallingManual tool-schema projectionAutomated tool execution and registry


Sources: packages/llm/src/protocols/gemini.ts146-162 packages/http-recorder/README.md1-5
